title: layia glandulosa var. hispida
intro: Layia glandulosa var. hispida, also known as the hispid layia or hispid tidy tips, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family, Asteraceae. The plant is primarily known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. Layia glandulosa var. hispida has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, making it a valuable herbal remedy for overall well-being. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
